---

## Unable to Confirm a Hike Took Place

The only GPS activity today was a single trackpoint at 21:55 UTC — too sparse to form a session at all, so it can't be classified as a hike either way (not enough points to determine a movement pattern, walking or otherwise). No other GPS-active period was detected today.

That doesn't mean nothing happened — see below.

---

## What Did Happen Today

Seven voice observations were logged in a tight 55-minute window starting around 14:04 UTC (7:04 AM local): a Palo Verde's leaf trail (mistaken at first for an ant trail), blooming desert plants smelling "wonderful," a second leaf trail from a creosote bush, a jackrabbit sighting, a few sprinkles of rain, and an unusual green scat find. That reads as a real walk — just not one the hiking-monitor device (or GPSLogger) was actively tracking.

The hiking-monitor itself produced **zero** Environmental Data readings all day. The same shared sheet does have 288 readings from `front-porch-temp-sensor` today, so the pipeline itself is clearly working — this looks specific to the hiking-monitor device, not a data-pipeline problem. Worth a direct check: is the device currently deployed, charged, and powered on?
